brief_justification: Correctly identifies C with valid formula and conditional reasoning; explores multi-level implications including Jensen's inequality for non-uniform distributions and distinguishes mathematical vs. real-world framing; explicitly applies injected suppress/amplify signals and flags the uniformity assumption before verifying it; verifies by testing all four answer choices against boundary conditions (Jensen's inequality as a stress test); clearly separates the mathematical idealization (uniform assumption) from empirical reality (seasonal birth clustering) with explicit confidence qualification; systematically evaluates all four answer choices with specific mechanistic reasons for rejection; table mapping plus formula plus per-option derivation provides full reproducibility; avoids surface-similarity trap by probing whether structural isomorphism holds under the uniformity condition rather than accepting nominal parameter match; shows zero suppressed failure modes (no surface bias, no domain lock, no structure blindness) and explicitly flags uniformity as the critical condition; follows identify-structure → extract-topology → compare-domains → verify-boundary-gate path but response is largely linear rather than reflecting the FORK/JOIN branching structure explicitly.
